title: How to Schedule GBP Posts in GoHighLevel — Boost Local Visibility
description: Learn how to schedule GBP posts in GoHighLevel to boost local visibility. Step-by-step guide to automate Google Business Profile posting across multiple locations.
html_content:
Manually posting to Google Business Profile for every location is killing your productivity. You're juggling multiple tabs, posting at random times, and watching competitors rank higher because their listings stay fresh and active. There's a better way. With GoHighLevel's GBP Post Scheduler, you can batch-create posts, schedule them weeks in advance, and manage your entire Google Business presence from one dashboard—without leaving the platform. This isn't just about saving time; it's about keeping every location visible, relevant, and ranked higher in local search results. Ready to automate your way to better local visibility? Let's dive in.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Is the GoHighLevel GBP Post Scheduler?
  • Why Scheduling GBP Posts Matters for Local SEO
  • Step-by-Step: How to Schedule GBP Posts in GoHighLevel
  • Using AI to Generate Post Content Instantly
  • Managing Your GBP Posting Calendar
  • Best Practices for Maximum Local Visibility

What Is the GoHighLevel GBP Post Scheduler?

The GBP Post Scheduler is a fully integrated tool within GoHighLevel's Social Planner that streamlines how you create, schedule, and publish posts directly to your Google Business Profile. Instead of toggling between multiple platforms, managing different dashboards, or trying to remember posting schedules, everything lives in one centralized hub.

This tool lets you:

  • Create and schedule Updates, Offers, and Events for single or multiple locations at once
  • Bulk-publish posts across your entire portfolio of Google Business listings
  • Use AI-powered content generation to write compelling post copy in seconds
  • Preview posts before they go live to ensure brand consistency
  • Manage all scheduled posts from a unified calendar and dashboard

If you're running a multi-location business or managing Google Business Profiles for multiple clients, this is a game-changer. You're no longer at the mercy of manual posting or outdated spreadsheets tracking post schedules.

Why Scheduling GBP Posts Matters for Local SEO

Google rewards businesses that keep their profiles active and engaging. The algorithm favors fresh, consistent content. When you post regularly to your Google Business Profile, you signal to Google that your business is legitimate, updated, and worth showing to local searchers.

Here's what happens when you schedule GBP posts consistently:

  • Improved local search rankings: Active profiles rank higher in local pack results and map listings
  • Increased customer engagement: Posts appear in local search results and on your profile, inviting clicks and visits
  • More qualified traffic: Posts drive calls, direction requests, and website clicks from high-intent local customers
  • Competitive edge: Most competitors aren't posting regularly; consistent activity sets you apart
  • Brand authority: Regular posts about promotions, events, and updates build trust and relevance

Without scheduling automation, most businesses fall off the posting wagon within a few weeks. With GoHighLevel's scheduler, you can batch 8 weeks of posts in one session, then sit back while they publish automatically at optimal times.

Step-by-Step: How to Schedule GBP Posts in GoHighLevel

Step 1: Access the Social Planner

Log into your GoHighLevel dashboard and navigate to the Social Planner from the main menu. This is where all your social media and GBP posting happens.

Step 2: Select Google Business Profile as Your Destination

Click the option to create a new post and choose Google Business Profile as your destination platform. You'll see a dropdown to select which location(s) you want to post to—select one or multiple locations.

Step 3: Choose Your Post Type

Google Business allows three types of posts:

  • Updates: General news, announcements, or reminders about your business
  • Offers: Promotions, discounts, or special deals to drive conversions
  • Events: Upcoming events, webinars, or time-sensitive activities

Select the post type that fits your content.

Step 4: Write or Generate Your Post Content

Type your post copy directly into the composer. Include a clear call-to-action, relevant keywords, and compelling language that encourages clicks or visits. Keep it concise—Google Business posts work best when they're scannable and action-oriented.

Step 5: Add Images (Optional but Recommended)

Upload a high-quality image or video. Posts with visuals get more engagement and stand out in local search results. Use bright, professional images that represent your business or the specific offer.

Step 6: Preview Your Post

The right side of the composer shows a real-time preview of how your post will appear on Google Business Profile. Check for formatting, typos, and overall appearance before scheduling.

Step 7: Schedule for Later or Post Now

Instead of clicking "Post," click "Schedule for Later" to choose a date and time. Select your optimal posting window (typically Tuesday–Thursday, 10 AM–2 PM for local businesses) and confirm.

Step 8: View Your Calendar

Your scheduled posts appear in the Social Planner calendar at the bottom of the page. You can see all upcoming posts across all locations in one view, edit them before they publish, or delete if plans change.

💡 Pro Tip

Batch your post creation. Spend 30–45 minutes writing 8–12 posts at once, then schedule them across 2–3 months. This eliminates the daily "what do I post?" friction and keeps your calendar full without constant effort.

Using AI to Generate Post Content Instantly

Staring at a blank screen trying to write another post? GoHighLevel's built-in AI content generator can create post copy for you in seconds. This is a massive productivity multiplier, especially if you're managing multiple locations or client accounts.

How to Use AI Content Generation:

While composing your post, look for the AI Generate button. Click it and choose your post type (Update, Offer, or Event). The AI will ask for a few details:

  • Your business type or industry
  • The specific topic or promotion you want to highlight
  • Your target audience or tone preference

Within seconds, you'll get a fully written, ready-to-use post. You can refine it further, adjust the tone, or regenerate if you want something different. The AI-generated copy is always on-brand, keyword-aware, and optimized for engagement.

This feature alone can save 5–10 hours per month if you're managing multiple locations or client portfolios.

Managing Your GBP Posting Calendar

Once your posts are scheduled, managing them is straightforward. The Social Planner dashboard gives you a bird's-eye view of your entire posting strategy.

Calendar View: See all your scheduled posts laid out by date. This helps you avoid posting too frequently (which can feel spammy) or leaving gaps (which hurts visibility). Aim for 2–3 posts per location per week for optimal results.

Edit Before Publishing: If you need to change a post before it goes live, click on it in the calendar and edit the content, image, or schedule time. Changes save automatically.

Bulk Management: If you're managing multiple locations, you can see all posts across all locations in one place. This prevents inconsistencies and ensures every location gets regular attention.

Performance Tracking: GoHighLevel shows you engagement metrics on published posts, so you can see which types of content resonate with your local audience and adjust future posts accordingly.

Best Practices for Maximum Local Visibility

Post Frequency: Aim for 2–3 posts per location per week. This keeps your profile fresh without overwhelming your audience. Consistent posting signals to Google that your business is active.

Mix Post Types: Don't post only Updates. Rotate between Updates, Offers, and Events to keep content diverse and engaging. Offers and Events typically drive more clicks than general updates.

Include Local Keywords: Mention your city, neighborhood, or service area in your posts. "Serving [City Name] since 2010" or "[Service] specialists for the [Neighborhood] area" helps local SEO.

Use High-Quality Images: Posts with images get 35% more engagement. Use professional photos that clearly represent your business or offer—avoid stock photos when possible.

Add a Clear Call-to-Action: Tell people what to do next. "Call now," "Learn more," "Book your appointment," or "Visit our website" drives actual results instead of just impressions.

Post at Optimal Times: Tuesday–Thursday between 10 AM–2 PM typically sees the highest engagement for local businesses. GoHighLevel lets you schedule posts at specific times to hit these windows automatically.

Promote Local Events and Offers: Posts about limited-time offers, local events, or seasonal promotions get higher engagement and drive qualified foot traffic. Make these your priority posts.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I schedule GBP posts for multiple locations at once?

Yes. When creating a post, you can select multiple locations from your portfolio and schedule the same post (or variations) to all of them simultaneously. This is perfect for network-wide promotions or announcements that apply across multiple branches.

What's the best posting frequency for Google Business Profile?

2–3 posts per location per week is ideal. This keeps your profile active and engaged without overwhelming your audience. More frequent posting can actually hurt engagement if posts seem spammy. The key is consistency—regular posting over weeks and months matters more than sporadic bursts.

Can I edit a post after it's been scheduled?

Absolutely. In the Social Planner calendar, click on any scheduled post to edit its content, images, or scheduled time. You can make changes right up until the post publishes automatically. This gives you flexibility if plans change or you want to refine messaging.

Does GoHighLevel track GBP post performance?

Yes. After posts publish, GoHighLevel shows you engagement metrics including views, clicks, phone calls, direction requests, and website visits attributed to each post. This data helps you understand what types of content your local audience responds to best.

What types of posts perform best on Google Business Profile?

Offer and Event posts typically outperform general Updates by 50%+ because they give people a reason to act immediately. Posts with images also see higher engagement. Promote limited-time offers, seasonal specials, and upcoming events for best results.
